agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery with a time-bound SLA, premium fee, and refund policy for missed windows. Raw requirements gathered from five stakeholder groups with conflicting positions on core terms.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery — a sub-domain of Order Fulfillment covering express order eligibility, dispatch, SLA tracking, and refund processing.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Express Delivery","definition":"A premium delivery option with a stated 2-hour delivery window and higher fee, available to eligible customers."},{"term":"Express Fee","definition":"The $12.99 surcharge applied on top of the base delivery fee for express service."},{"term":"Base Delivery Fee","definition":"The standard delivery fee charged regardless of delivery speed tier."},{"term":"Delivery Window","definition":"The 2-hour time period starting from order placement within which the express delivery should be completed."},{"term":"Pickup SLA","definition":"The 15-minute maximum time allowed between order placement and driver pickup at the warehouse."},{"term":"Express Eligibility","definition":"The set of conditions (distance, warehouse proximity) a customer must meet to select express delivery."},{"term":"Eligible Driver","definition":"A driver with a minimum 4-star rating who is qualified to fulfill express orders."},{"term":"Replacement Assignment","definition":"The process of assigning a new driver when the original express driver cancels mid-delivery, with a 10-minute target."},{"term":"Goodwill Refund","definition":"A refund of the express fee issued when the delivery window is missed — the nature of this refund (automatic right vs. discretionary gesture) is currently in dispute between stakeholders."},{"term":"Wrong Address Exception","definition":"A refund exclusion triggered when the customer provided an incorrect delivery address."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Express delivery is only available to customers located within 10km of a warehouse.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Physical constraint — the 2-hour window is logistically impossible beyond this radius. Allowing ineligible orders corrupts the SLA.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer address, WHEN the distance to the nearest warehouse exceeds 10km, THEN the express delivery option must not be selectable."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"The express fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Pricing is a business policy that can be changed by finance or product leadership.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer selects express delivery, WHEN the order is confirmed, THEN the total includes a $12.99 express surcharge on top of the base delivery fee."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Express orders must be picked up from the warehouse within 15 minutes of order placement.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Operations has stated the 2-hour window cannot be met if pickup exceeds 15 minutes. This is a hard operational constraint that protects SLA integrity.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express order is placed, WHEN 15 minutes elapse without pickup, THEN the system must escalate (alert ops, attempt reassignment)."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Only drivers with a rating of 4 stars or above may be assigned to express orders.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Rating threshold is a quality policy that ops leadership could adjust.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver with a rating below 4.0, WHEN the dispatch system considers them for an express order, THEN they must be excluded from the candidate pool."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"If the express delivery window is missed, the express fee is refunded but the base delivery fee is not.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Refund scope is a business policy, and the nature of the refund itself is disputed (see escalations).","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express delivery is completed after the 2-hour window, WHEN a refund is triggered, THEN only the $12.99 express fee is refunded, not the base delivery fee."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"No refund is issued if the customer provided a wrong delivery address.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a policy carve-out that could be revised.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express delivery missed its window, WHEN the root cause is an incorrect customer-provided address, THEN no express fee refund is issued."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Refunds for missed express windows are processed within 3 business days.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Processing timeline is a finance policy, not a physical or data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a refund is approved for a missed express window, WHEN the refund is initiated, THEN it must be completed within 3 business days."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"If a driver cancels mid-delivery, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes or the express fee is automatically refunded.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Replacement window and auto-refund trigger are operational policies.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express driver cancels mid-delivery, WHEN 10 minutes elapse without a replacement being assigned, THEN the system automatically initiates an express fee refund."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"Customers can track their express driver in real time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Feature requirement, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express order is in transit, WHEN the customer views the order, THEN real-time driver location is displayed."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer is more than 10km from any warehouse","when":"They attempt to select express delivery","then":"The express option is disabled with a message explaining the distance requirement"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer is within 10km of a warehouse and selects express delivery","when":"The order is confirmed","then":"The order total includes a $12.99 express surcharge and the 2-hour delivery window start time is recorded"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An express order has been placed","when":"No driver has picked up the order within 15 minutes","then":"The system escalates to operations and attempts to reassign to another eligible driver"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An express delivery is completed outside the 2-hour window","when":"The customer's address was verified as correct","then":"The $12.99 express fee is refunded within 3 business days; the base delivery fee is not refunded"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An express delivery is completed outside the 2-hour window","when":"The delay was caused by an incorrect customer-provided address","then":"No refund is issued"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An express driver cancels mid-delivery","when":"No replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes","then":"The express fee refund is automatically initiated"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An express order is in transit","when":"The customer views the order details","then":"The driver's real-time location and the remaining time in the 2-hour window are displayed"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"The dispatch system is selecting a driver for an express order","when":"A candidate driver has a rating below 4.0 stars","then":"That driver is excluded from the express candidate pool"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Customer is exactly 10km from the warehouse","expected_behavior":"Boundary condition — must define whether 10km is inclusive or exclusive. Recommend inclusive (<=10km is eligible)."},{"scenario":"Driver rating drops below 4.0 stars during an active express delivery","expected_behavior":"The current delivery should be completed by the assigned driver. The rating threshold applies only at dispatch time, not mid-delivery."},{"scenario":"No eligible drivers (4+ stars) are available within the 15-minute pickup window","expected_behavior":"The system should alert operations. Policy decision needed: does the order convert to standard delivery with express fee waived, or does it wait?"},{"scenario":"Customer provides a wrong address but corrects it within minutes","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous — does the 'wrong address' exception apply only when the wrong address caused the delay, or any time a wrong address was initially entered?"},{"scenario":"Driver cancels at minute 9 and a replacement is assigned at minute 11 but completes within the original 2-hour window","expected_behavior":"The 10-minute replacement SLA was breached, but the delivery window was met. Unclear whether the auto-refund still triggers."},{"scenario":"Multiple driver cancellations on the same express order","expected_behavior":"Does the 10-minute replacement clock reset with each cancellation? At what point is the order auto-refunded regardless?"},{"scenario":"Warehouse is temporarily out of stock after express order is placed","expected_behavior":"Not addressed in any stakeholder notes. Refund policy and SLA handling for supply-side failures is undefined."},{"scenario":"Customer changes delivery address after order placement to a location >10km from warehouse","expected_behavior":"Should express eligibility be re-evaluated? If now ineligible, does the order downgrade to standard with a fee adjustment?"}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Express fee at $12.99 creates a new revenue stream. However, the refund policy (especially the auto-refund on driver cancellation) could create significant cost exposure if driver cancellation rates are high. The 3-business-day refund processing timeline has cash flow implications.","operational":"The 15-minute pickup SLA and 10-minute replacement SLA impose tight operational constraints. Requires real-time dispatch optimization, driver availability monitoring, and potentially dedicated express driver pools. The 4-star rating filter reduces the eligible driver pool.","user":"Real-time tracking and a prominent delivery window create strong customer expectations. The contradiction between 'guarantee' messaging and 'best effort' legal reality is the single biggest UX risk — overpromising leads to trust erosion.","risk":"HIGH — The guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction is a legal and brand risk. Marketing 'guaranteed 2-hour delivery' while legal terms say 'best effort' exposes the company to false advertising claims and regulatory scrutiny. Additionally, the auto-refund trigger on driver cancellation could be exploited.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Expect a guaranteed 2-hour delivery based on PM/CS messaging. If legal softens this to 'best effort,' customer satisfaction and trust depend entirely on how this is communicated."},{"group":"Drivers","impact":"4-star minimum creates a performance gate. Express orders likely carry higher pressure. Compensation model for express not discussed — key gap."},{"group":"Operations","impact":"Must staff and dispatch to meet aggressive 15-minute pickup and 10-minute replacement SLAs. Need tooling for real-time SLA monitoring."},{"group":"Finance","impact":"Must process refunds within 3 business days. Need fraud detection for wrong-address exception gaming and driver-cancellation auto-refund abuse."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Directly affected by how the delivery window is communicated. Must review all customer-facing language to avoid liability from the word 'guarantee.'"},{"group":"Customer Success","impact":"Will handle disputes when 'guarantee' expectations clash with 'best effort' reality. Need clear escalation scripts and refund authority."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The spec contains a direct, unresolved contradiction between Product/CS ('guaranteed') and Legal ('best effort / goodwill') on the core value proposition. This is not a minor detail — it affects how the product is marketed, what obligations are created, whether refunds are automatic rights or discretionary, and how every customer-facing surface is worded. Until this is resolved, the team cannot build confidently. Secondary gaps (driver compensation, distance measurement method, stock-out handling) further reduce confidence.","high_confidence_areas":["Express fee amount ($12.99)","Distance eligibility threshold (10km from warehouse)","Pickup SLA (15 minutes)","Driver rating minimum (4.0 stars)","Wrong address refund exclusion","Refund scope (express fee only, not base fee)"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether 10km is straight-line distance or driving distance","Driver compensation model for express orders not mentioned by any stakeholder","Stock-out or warehouse-side failure handling completely unaddressed","Whether the 2-hour window starts at order placement or at pickup","How 'wrong address' is determined and by whom","Fraud prevention for auto-refund triggers"],"assumptions":["The 2-hour delivery window starts at order placement, not at pickup, based on the Operations Lead's concern that pickup delays eat into the window","The 10km distance is measured as straight-line (geodesic) distance, as driving distance would require real-time routing","The 4-star rating is a lifetime/rolling average, not a recent-period rating"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"GUARANTEE vs. BEST EFFORT: Product Manager and Customer Success describe the 2-hour delivery as a 'guarantee' with automatic refunds for missed windows. Legal explicitly states this cannot be a guarantee in writing — it is a 'best effort' SLA and the refund is a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' These two positions are mutually exclusive and affect every customer-facing surface, the refund policy, and system behavior.","affected_stakeholders":["Product Management","Legal","Customer Success","Customers","Marketing"],"options":["A) Side with Legal: Position as 'best effort' with discretionary goodwill refunds. Refunds are not automatic — they require review.","B) Side with PM/CS: Position as a guarantee with automatic refunds, accepting the legal risk and adjusting terms of service accordingly.","C) Hybrid: Use 'estimated' or 'target' language externally while implementing automatic refunds internally as a business policy (not a legal obligation). Legal terms say best effort; product behavior acts like a guarantee."],"recommendation":"Option C — Hybrid approach. Externally use language like 'Express: Delivery target within 2 hours' rather than 'guaranteed.' Internally, implement automatic refunds as business policy, giving customers the guarantee experience without the legal exposure. Legal must review and approve all customer-facing copy. This requires a dedicated alignment session between PM, Legal, and CS before any implementation begins."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"AUTOMATIC REFUND vs. GOODWILL GESTURE: Customer Success requires automatic refund if replacement driver is not assigned within 10 minutes. Finance describes refund processing within 3 business days (implying a review process). Legal says refunds are goodwill, not enforceable. These three positions conflict on whether refunds are automatic, processed, or discretionary.","affected_stakeholders":["Customer Success","Finance","Legal","Customers"],"options":["A) Fully automatic: System triggers refund with no human review.","B) Semi-automatic: System flags for refund, finance reviews within 3 business days.","C) Manual: Customer must request refund, CS reviews against policy."],"recommendation":"Option B — Semi-automatic with a 3-business-day SLA for finance review. The system auto-flags eligible refunds, finance processes them. This satisfies the operational need for speed while giving finance oversight and legal the discretionary framing. CS gets clear criteria for customer communication."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Cannot proceed with implementation until the guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction is resolved — this affects the core product promise, all UI copy, refund logic, and SLA monitoring behavior.","business_rule_broken":"The system cannot simultaneously encode BR-5 (automatic refund on missed window) and Legal's position that refunds are discretionary goodwill gestures. These produce different system behaviors."}]}

Raw requirements gathered from five stakeholder groups with conflicting positions on core terms.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery — a sub-domain of Order Fulfillment covering express order eligibility, dispatch, SLA tracking, and refund processing.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Express Delivery","definition":"A premium de

content.thinking:
Let me analyze these workshop notes carefully for the Express Delivery product.

The key contradiction I see: Product Manager says "guaranteed within 2 hours" while Legal says "We cannot guarantee a 2-hour delivery time in writing — this is a 'best effort' SLA, not a contractual guarantee." This is a direct contradiction between two explicit stakeholder positions.

Let me also look for other issues:

1. PM says "guaranteed" vs Legal says "best effort SLA" — direct contradiction
2. PM says refund if missed window, Legal says refund is "goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable" — this creates tension around whether refund is automatic/guaranteed or discretionary


3. Customer Success says "refund automatically" if replacement not assigned within 10 minutes — but Legal says refund is goodwill, not enforceable
4. Finance says no refund for wrong address — this is a clear carve-out

The primary blocker is the contradiction between PM and Customer Success explicitly guaranteeing refunds while Legal explicitly says they cannot guarantee this in writing. Since this is a contradiction, confidence must be 50 or lower, and all escalation types must be marked as "contradiction."

I'm also noting some ambiguities that lower confidence: the distance calculation between the 10km threshold and warehouse isn't specified as straight-line or driving distance, and the refund process for the automatic refund trigger is incomplete.
